summary: Closed, unmerged workflow/QoL PR that reworked `.github/workflows/editorconfig.yml` to make the EditorConfig check more stable and reusable. The proposed workflow replaced Nix-installed `editorconfig-checker` with a fixed-tag official container image, pinned the runner to `ubuntu-22.04`, removed a hardcoded `doldecomp` repository-owner condition, updated file exclusions, ran the checker in verbose-style direct command mode, and added a GitHub Actions step summary listing checked files. The PR body says this was meant to close #746 and avoid future surprises from changing checker installs; a later comment says it was repurposed as a QoL fix.
what_changed: Renamed the workflow from `Checking EditorConfig` to `Check EditorConfig`.
Changed the job runner from `ubuntu-latest` to the fixed image `ubuntu-22.04`.
Removed the workflow guard requiring `github.repository_owner == 'doldecomp'`, leaving only the `[skip editorconfig]` title opt-out.
Replaced the Nix install path for `editorconfig-checker` with a Docker-based extraction of `/usr/bin/ec` from `mstruebing/editorconfig-checker:2.7.0`.
Changed checkout ref handling from `refs/pull/${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}/merge` to `${{ github.head_ref }}` because `pull_request_target` checks out the base branch by default.
Moved changed-file discovery into the same step that runs the checker.
Generalized the GitHub API path from `repos/doldecomp/melee/...` to `repos/${{github.repository_owner}}/melee/...`.
Updated the changed-file exclusion regex from only excluding `tools/m2c` to excluding `dump`, `build`, `expected`, `tools/m2c`, and `tools/calcprogress`.
Changed the changed-files temp file path from `$HOME/changed_files` to `$HOME/changed_files.txt`.
Ran `/tmp/ec -disable-indent-size` through `xargs -r` against the filtered changed-file list.
Added a `GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY` markdown section listing files changed and checked by EditorConfig.
Kept the failure message that points contributors to EditorConfig setup documentation.
smart_moves: Pinned both the runner version and checker image tag to reduce CI drift from upstream changes.
Using a container image for the checker avoided depending on a pinned Nixpkgs revision and a separate Nix install step.
Removing hardcoded `doldecomp` references made the workflow more usable in forks or non-primary repositories.
The updated exclusion filter avoided wasting editorconfig checks on generated or expected-output directories.
Adding a step summary made CI output easier for contributors to audit without digging through raw logs.
The `[skip editorconfig]` title escape hatch was preserved, which is useful for PRs intentionally touching formatting-sensitive or generated files.
decomp_lessons: Even decompilation repositories benefit from deterministic CI tooling: pin external linters/checkers and runner versions when possible.
Generated artifacts and comparison outputs such as `dump`, `build`, and `expected` should usually be excluded from style checks unless the check is explicitly intended for them.
Workflow logic should avoid hardcoded organization names when the same checks need to run on forks or mirrored repositories.

For pull-request workflows, checkout ref semantics matter; the diff notes that `pull_request_target` checks out the base branch by default, so an explicit PR/head ref is required when checking contributor changes.
CI changes should improve contributor feedback, not only pass/fail status; listing exactly which files were checked is reusable practice.
naming_conventions: Workflow display name was simplified from gerund form `Checking EditorConfig` to imperative/noun phrase `Check EditorConfig`.
Step names followed concise imperative descriptions such as `Get editorconfig-checker command from image` and `Check EditorConfig`.
The temporary changed-file list was named `$HOME/changed_files.txt`, making the file type clearer than `$HOME/changed_files`.
review_feedback: No formal review comments were present in the provided slice.

The author commented that the PR was repurposed as a QoL fix and listed specific improvements: fixed Ubuntu version, removal of hardcoded `doldecomp` references, updated `grep` exclude filter, verbose checker mode, and markdown step summary.
The PR was closed without merge according to the provided context, so these changes should not be assumed to have landed.
follow_up_queries: Check issue #746 for the original CI breakage or motivation behind replacing the editorconfig-checker install path.
Search later PRs for whether the container-image approach or parts of this workflow were eventually merged.
Audit current `.github/workflows/editorconfig.yml` for whether it still hardcodes repository owner or uses drifting tool versions.
Verify whether `github.repository_owner` is sufficient for fork PRs or whether `${{ github.repository }}` would be safer if repository names differ.
